steps.title: Find the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence says axial tilt is not the only factor that affects the Sun's position. The word though interrupts that idea before the dependent as-clause explains another factor: the eccentricity of Earth's orbit.

steps.title: Set off though
steps.content: When though is used parenthetically in the middle of a sentence, it needs commas on both sides. A semicolon would not work before the as-clause because that clause cannot stand alone as a complete sentence.

steps.title: Pick the punctuation
steps.content:
• Choice A: Uses a comma before and after though.
  • Choice B: Uses a semicolon before a dependent clause.
  • Choice C: Leaves the parenthetical though unclosed.
  • Choice D: Also uses a semicolon before a dependent clause.
The answer is A.
